title: Market endures panamax shock during difficult week
section_path: Market endures panamax shock during difficult week
section_path_text: Market endures panamax shock during difficult week
summary:
Capesize
Yet another depressing week for capesizes with rates slipping again in the East and in the Atlantic. Too many ships in the East continued to weigh on the market and although some of the majors have been taking ships for West Australia, each has been at successively lower rates. As the week closed out the West Australia/Qingdao rate stood at $4.15 for 170,000-tonnes 10% for 11 February eta.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: Yet another depressing week for capesizes with rates slipping again in the East and in the Atlantic. Too many ships in the East continued to weigh on the market and although some of the majors have been taking ships for West Australia, each has been at successively lower rates.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: Fronthaul business fixed with no ballast bonuses from the US Gulf to the East shocked the market this week. There were mitigating circumstances - early committed ships needing to go east and in one instance for drydock.

children.title: Supramax / Handysize
children.summary: Another very tough week for the owners as the market in all areas continued to crumble. Activity was limited in the US Gulf although a nice 61,400 dwt 2011 built ‘ultra' type open US Atlantic was paid a relatively encouraging $12,500 daily for a trip to the east Mediterranean by a charterer who had an early position to cover.
